author: brandon
body: Darren - read this before Thursday's panel job. Verify dead, every time.

author: brandon
body: Added the torque specs to the lug step. Mark each one with the paint pen.

author: darren
body: Got it. Where do we keep the spare locks for tagout?

author: brandon
body: Side bin in the van, blue case. Take two so you always have a backup.

author: brandon
body: Print this and keep it in the van. Inspector asks every job.

author: brandon
body: Walk this list the night before. Fix the small stuff then.

author: darren
body: On the EV job the unit had GFCI built in. So no GFCI breaker, right?

author: brandon
body: Right. Built-in covers it. Standard breaker. Check the nameplate first.

author: brandon
body: Fiberglass only near panels. I saw the aluminum ladder in the van. Out it goes.

author: brandon
body: Memorize the common sizes. I will quiz you Monday.

author: darren
body: The dead bedroom outlet was a backstab that let go. Found it second device in.

author: brandon
body: Good. Pigtail those instead of backstabbing. Lasts longer.

author: brandon
body: Neutrals float in the sub. This is the one everybody gets wrong. Do not.

author: brandon
body: Check the van Monday morning and update the board. Ran out of AFCI breakers last week.

author: darren
body: Worked through the meter readings on the service call. Confirmed dead before I opened it.

author: brandon
body: Nail plates on the at-risk runs before inspector gets there. He writes those up every time.

author: darren
body: K and T job - found cloth wire taped into the hall fixture. Left it for you to look at.

author: brandon
body: Good call. Never splice onto K and T. We pull the whole run on Monday.

author: brandon
body: Get the cut order number from PECO and put it in the job folder. They ask every time.

author: brandon
body: Bond the water pipe within 5 feet of entry. We missed that on the last final.

author: darren
body: Ran the load calc on the EV quote. 100A service is full. Customer needs the upgrade first.

author: brandon
body: That is the right answer. No permit, no shortcut. Customer pushed - bring those to me.
